lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Hello! What can I do for you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: I'm Marwolaeth, bringer of prophecy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's nice to meet you Marwolaeth, I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: And it's great to meet you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: I'm the local wisdom, my dreams are those of prophecy. Would you care for a reading?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, tell me of my future...

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: I see a blinding light! A fire so big the heavens can see it; all those who look upon the great flame struggle to look away.

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: I see a darkness so thick, light fails to escape it - A shadow world where up is down and right is wrong.

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: I see peace and prosperity - The air is filled with wonderous bird song and the scent of earth and river.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: It isn't my place to derive meaning. Allow my foretelling to guide your future but do not rely upon it happening all by itself. We are the arbiters of our own destiny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's really quite vague, do you just say this to everyone?

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: It's your choice to believe, not mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, alright... Thanks, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: You're most welcome, [player name].

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Was there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, no thanks...

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd prefer to stay ignorant... Who knows what that knowledge could do to a person...

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: A blissful existence may be the correct path.

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Was there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about this place?

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: I can! This is the quaint town of Gwenith. All those who have been granted access to the great crystal city of Prifddinas are welcome here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with that creepy mountain?

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Ah... It is expected for an outsider like yourself to notice the mountain wonder how we exist in its shadow.

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: We leave the mountain alone and it lets us live peacefully, out of sight of any passing boats.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It? The mountain is conscious?

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Of course! At the very top of the mountain sits a ruin. Whatever calamity happened there; their ghosts remain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: G-g-ghosts?!

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Yes. Explore at your own peril, [player name]. There are those who have looked for answers from these mynydd spirits. They never return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't necessarily mean they died... They may have found the meaning that they desired and have gone elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Elsewhere? Indeed... As I said, [player name], explore at your own peril. Was there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Marwolaeth Dawnsio
lines.text: Farewell, adventurer.
